Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Isuzu River (Mie)|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|qq=draft|qq=draft}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the {{ill|Itagaki (sacred fence)|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 23 (Japan)|ja|国道23号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0106.html Takimatsuri-no-Kami] - Ise Jingu Worship Association * [http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/t0003.php Takimatsuri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoito Kose] - {{ill|ITV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

{{神社 |名称= 滝祭神 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Takimatsuri-no-kami 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]宇治館町1 内宮宮域内 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 19.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 18.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 瀧祭大神 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}所管社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= なし |別名= おとりつぎさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= 由貴大御饌、[[神嘗祭]] }}
== Overview ==
'''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the {{ill|Itagaki (sacred fence)|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.

== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.

The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. == Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 23 (Japan)|ja|国道23号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.

The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 23 (Japan)|ja|国道23号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0106.html Takimatsuri-no-Kami] - Ise Jingu Worship Association * [http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/t0003.php Takimatsuri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoito Kose] - {{ill|ITV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 滝祭神 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Takimatsuri-no-kami 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]宇治館町1 内宮宮域内 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 19.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 18.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 瀧祭大神 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}所管社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= なし |別名= おとりつぎさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= 由貴大御饌、[[神嘗祭]] }}
== Overview ==
'''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Isuzu River (Mie)|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|qq=draft|qq=draft}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the {{ill|Itagaki (sacred fence)|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 23 (Japan)|ja|国道23号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0106.html Takimatsuri-no-Kami] - Ise Jingu Worship Association * [http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/t0003.php Takimatsuri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoito Kose] - {{ill|ITV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 23 (Japan)|ja|国道23号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0106.html Takimatsuri-no-Kami] - Ise Jingu Worship Association * [http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/t0003.php Takimatsuri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoito Kose] - {{ill|ITV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{125 Shrines of Ise}}
{{translated page|ja|滝祭神|version=64058165|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ise]] [[Category:125 Shrines of Ise]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Takimatsuri-no-Kami]] [[Category:Shikigeisha]] [[Category:日本の滝]] [[Category:宮川水系]] [[Category:Shrines in Ise City]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takimatsuri-no-Kami]][[Category:qq]] }}
[[sn:Takimatsuri-no-Kami]] [[ja:滝祭神]]
<!--Takimatsuri-no-Kami-->

{{神社 |名称= 滝祭神 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Takimatsuri-no-kami 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]宇治館町1 内宮宮域内 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 19.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 18.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 瀧祭大神 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}所管社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= なし |別名= おとりつぎさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= 由貴大御饌、[[神嘗祭]] }}
== Overview ==
'''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Isuzu River (Mie)|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|qq=draft|qq=draft}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the {{ill|Itagaki (sacred fence)|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 23 (Japan)|ja|国道23号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0106.html Takimatsuri-no-Kami] - Ise Jingu Worship Association * [http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/t0003.php Takimatsuri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoito Kose] - {{ill|ITV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 滝祭神 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Takimatsuri-no-kami 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]宇治館町1 内宮宮域内 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 19.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 18.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 瀧祭大神 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}所管社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= なし |別名= おとりつぎさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= 由貴大御饌、[[神嘗祭]] }}
== Overview ==
'''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of the {{ill|Isuzu River (Mie)|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|qq=draft|qq=draft}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the {{ill|Itagaki (sacred fence)|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 23 (Japan)|ja|国道23号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0106.html Takimatsuri-no-Kami] - Ise Jingu Worship Association * [http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/t0003.php Takimatsuri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoito Kose] - {{ill|ITV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{125 Shrines of Ise}}

== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} via {{ill|National Route 23 (Japan)|ja|国道23号|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== References == <references /> == External Links == * [http://www.jingukaikan.jp/125mairi/m01/0106.html Takimatsuri-no-Kami] - Ise Jingu Worship Association * [http://www.yoitokose.jp/ise/encyclopedia/t0003.php Takimatsuri-no-Kami | Ise Jingu Encyclopedia | Ise-Shima Travel Yoito Kose] - {{ill|ITV|ja|アイティービー|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 滝祭神 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Takimatsuri-no-kami 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]宇治館町1 内宮宮域内 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 19.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 18.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 瀧祭大神 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}所管社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= なし |別名= おとりつぎさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= 由貴大御饌、[[神嘗祭]] }}
== Overview ==
'''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarashi|qq=}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|qq=}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|qq=}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}}, Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/water deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin|qq=}} of the {{ill|Isuzu River (Mie)|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu River (Mie)|qq=}}, which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|qq=}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the {{ill|Itagaki (sacred fence)|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itagaki (sacred fence)|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/source of the Isuzu River|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood control|qq=}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishikichō|qq=}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} or the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikkokushi|qq=}}, making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū Annual Rituals|qq=}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}} in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}} and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|qq=}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}}, a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naorai|qq=}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto priest)|qq=}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujibashi Bridge|qq=}} * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Sangū Line|qq=}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugawa Station|qq=}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=}} via {{ill|National Route 23 (Japan)|ja|国道23号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 23 (Japan)|qq=}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoke Bridge|qq=}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}}, Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|qq=}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the {{ill|Itagaki (sacred fence)|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itagaki (sacred fence)|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/source of the Isuzu River|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood control|qq=}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishikichō|qq=}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} or the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikkokushi|qq=}}, making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū Annual Rituals|qq=}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}} in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}} and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|qq=}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}}, a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naorai|qq=}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto priest)|qq=}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujibashi Bridge|qq=}} * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Sangū Line|qq=}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugawa Station|qq=}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=}} via {{ill|National Route 23 (Japan)|ja|国道23号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 23 (Japan)|qq=}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoke Bridge|qq=}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.

<!--Takimatsuri-no-Kami-->

{{神社 |名称= 滝祭神 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Takimatsuri-no-kami 01.jpg|270px]] |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]宇治館町1 内宮宮域内 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 19.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 18.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 瀧祭大神 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}所管社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= なし |別名= おとりつぎさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= 由貴大御饌、[[神嘗祭]] }}
== Overview ==
'''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarashi|qq=}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|qq=}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|qq=}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}}, Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/water deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin|qq=}} of the {{ill|Isuzu River (Mie)|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu River (Mie)|qq=}}, which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|qq=}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the {{ill|Itagaki (sacred fence)|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itagaki (sacred fence)|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/source of the Isuzu River|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood control|qq=}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishikichō|qq=}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} or the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikkokushi|qq=}}, making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=}}<ref name="gs"/>.

The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|qq=}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.

The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the {{ill|Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}}, a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naorai|qq=}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. '''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}} (Naikū) of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarashi|qq=}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|qq=}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|qq=}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|qq=}}, {{ill|Ise City|ja|伊勢市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise City|qq=}}, Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the {{ill|water deity|ja|水神|1=Suijin|lt=water god|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/water deity|13=User:Immanuelle/Suijin|qq=}} of the {{ill|Isuzu River (Mie)|ja|五十鈴川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzu River (Mie)|qq=}}, which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|qq=}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the {{ill|Itagaki (sacred fence)|ja|板垣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itagaki (sacred fence)|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/source of the Isuzu River|qq=}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood control|qq=}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishikichō|qq=}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} or the {{ill|Rikkokushi|ja|六国史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rikkokushi|qq=}}, making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū Annual Rituals|qq=}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the {{ill|Saikū|ja|斎宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saikū|qq=}} in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}} and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|qq=}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the {{ill|Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai Festival|lt=Kannamesai|ja|神嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannamesai|qq=}}, a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naorai|qq=}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto priest)|qq=}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujibashi Bridge|qq=}} * About 15 minutes by {{ill|Mie Kotsu|ja|三重交通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Kotsu|qq=}} bus from the south exit (JR side) of {{ill|Ise Station|ja|伊勢市駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|JR Sangū Line|ja|参宮線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR Sangū Line|qq=}} or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from {{ill|Isuzugawa Station|ja|五十鈴川駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Isuzugawa Station|qq=}} on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|qq=}} on the {{ill|Ise Expressway|ja|伊勢自動車道|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Expressway|qq=}}, or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=}} via {{ill|National Route 23 (Japan)|ja|国道23号|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Route 23 (Japan)|qq=}}. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoke Bridge|qq=}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoke Bridge|qq=}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.
