{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17229063]] Wikidata name: Takimatsurinokami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 滝祭神 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Takimatsuri-no-kami 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]宇治館町1 内宮宮域内 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 19.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 18.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 瀧祭大神 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}所管社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= なし |別名= おとりつぎさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= 由貴大御饌、[[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.4555|N|136.721889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
== Overview ==
'''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the [[:en:Suijin|water god]] of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River (Mie)]], which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the [[:en:Itagaki|Itagaki (sacred fence)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/source of the Isuzu River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood control|qq=}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishikichō|qq=}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} or the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Rikkokushi]], making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū Annual Rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]] in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first [[:en:Torii|torii]] and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|qq=|1=Hōhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445508}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]], a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naorai|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto priest)|qq=|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujibashi Bridge|qq=|1=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997081}} * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit (JR side) of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|JR Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23 (Japan)]]. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoke Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17229063]] Wikidata name: Takimatsurinokami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 滝祭神 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Takimatsuri-no-kami 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]宇治館町1 内宮宮域内 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 19.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 18.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 瀧祭大神 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}所管社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= なし |別名= おとりつぎさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= 由貴大御饌、[[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.4555|N|136.721889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
== Overview ==
'''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the [[:en:Suijin|water god]] of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River (Mie)]], which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the [[:en:Itagaki|Itagaki (sacred fence)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/source of the Isuzu River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood control|qq=}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishikichō|qq=}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} or the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Rikkokushi]], making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū Annual Rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]] in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first [[:en:Torii|torii]] and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|qq=|1=Hōhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445508}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]], a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naorai|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto priest)|qq=|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujibashi Bridge|qq=|1=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997081}} * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit (JR side) of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|JR Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23 (Japan)]]. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoke Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Takimatsurinokami]][[ja:滝祭神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17229063]] Wikidata name: Takimatsurinokami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 滝祭神 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Takimatsuri-no-kami 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]宇治館町1 内宮宮域内 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 19.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 18.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 瀧祭大神 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}所管社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= なし |別名= おとりつぎさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= 由貴大御饌、[[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.4555|N|136.721889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
== Overview ==
'''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the [[:en:Suijin|water god]] of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River (Mie)]], which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the [[:en:Itagaki|Itagaki (sacred fence)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.

The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishikichō|qq=}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. <!--Takimatsuri-no-Kami--> [[Category:Miyagawa River System]] [[Category:Waterfalls of Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Takimatsurinokami}} {{wikidata link|Q17229063}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takimatsurinokami]][[ja:滝祭神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17229063]] Wikidata name: Takimatsurinokami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 滝祭神 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Takimatsuri-no-kami 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]宇治館町1 内宮宮域内 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 19.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 18.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 瀧祭大神 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}所管社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= なし |別名= おとりつぎさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= 由貴大御饌、[[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|qid=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.4555|N|136.721889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
== Overview ==
'''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the [[:en:Suijin|water god]] of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River (Mie)]], which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the [[:en:Itagaki|Itagaki (sacred fence)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/source of the Isuzu River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood control|qq=}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishikichō|qq=}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} or the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Rikkokushi]], making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū Annual Rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]] in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first [[:en:Torii|torii]] and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|qq=|1=Hōhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445508}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]], a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naorai|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto priest)|qq=|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujibashi Bridge|qq=|1=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997081}} * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit (JR side) of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|JR Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23 (Japan)]]. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoke Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.

== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujibashi Bridge|qq=|1=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997081}} * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit (JR side) of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|JR Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23 (Japan)]]. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoke Bridge|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takimatsurinokami]][[ja:滝祭神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17229063]] Wikidata name: Takimatsurinokami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 滝祭神 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Takimatsuri-no-kami 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]宇治館町1 内宮宮域内 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 19.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 18.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 瀧祭大神 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}所管社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= なし |別名= おとりつぎさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= 由貴大御饌、[[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.4555|N|136.721889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
== Overview ==
'''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarashi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the [[:en:Suijin|water god]] of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River (Mie)]], which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the [[:en:Itagaki|Itagaki (sacred fence)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/source of the Isuzu River|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood control|qq=}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishikichō|qq=}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} or the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Rikkokushi]], making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū Annual Rituals|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]] in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first [[:en:Torii|torii]] and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|qq=|1=Hōhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445508}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]], a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naorai|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto priest)|qq=|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.

Also accessible via bus from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|qq=|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|qq=|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|qq=|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23 (Japan)]]. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.

Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.

== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishikichō|qq=}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} or the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Rikkokushi]], making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]] in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first [[:en:Torii|torii]] and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|1=Hōhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445508}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]], a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naorai|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto priest)|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujibashi Bridge|1=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997081}} * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit (JR side) of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|JR Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23 (Japan)]]. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoke Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Takimatsurinokami]][[ja:滝祭神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17229063]] Wikidata name: Takimatsurinokami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:48, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building |名称= 滝祭神 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= File:Takimatsuri-no-kami 01.jpg |所在地= [[三重県]][[Ise, Mie]]宇治館町1 内宮宮域内 |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 27 | 緯度秒 = 19.8 |経度度 = 136 |経度分 = 43 | 経度秒 = 18.8 |地図= Japan Mie |祭神= 瀧祭大神 |社格= {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}所管社 |創建= [[延暦]]23rd year （[[804]]）以前 |本殿= なし |別名= おとりつぎさん |札所等= |例祭= |神事= 由貴大御饌、[[神嘗祭]] |prefecture= {{ill|Ise|WD=Q328067}} |coordinates= {{Coord|34.4555|N|136.721889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }}
== Overview ==
'''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the [[:en:Suijin|water god]] of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River (Mie)]], which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the [[:en:Itagaki|Itagaki (sacred fence)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/source of the Isuzu River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood control|qq=}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishikichō|qq=}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} or the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Rikkokushi]], making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}<ref name="gs"/>.

As the enshrined deity is the [[:en:Suijin|water god]] of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River (Mie)]], which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the [[:en:Itagaki|Itagaki (sacred fence)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/source of the Isuzu River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood control|qq=}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishikichō|qq=}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} or the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Rikkokushi]], making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]] in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first [[:en:Torii|torii]] and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|1=Hōhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445508}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]], a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naorai|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto priest)|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujibashi Bridge|1=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997081}} * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit (JR side) of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|JR Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23 (Japan)]]. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoke Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

== Overview ==
'''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the [[:en:Suijin|water god]] of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River (Mie)]], which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the [[:en:Itagaki|Itagaki (sacred fence)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/source of the Isuzu River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood control|qq=}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishikichō|qq=}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} or the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Rikkokushi]], making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]] in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first [[:en:Torii|torii]] and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|1=Hōhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445508}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]], a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naorai|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto priest)|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujibashi Bridge|1=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997081}} * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit (JR side) of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|JR Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23 (Japan)]]. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoke Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.

'''Takimatsuri-no-Kami''' (滝祭神) is a shrine under the jurisdiction of {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]]. It is located within the precincts of Naikū, near the {{ill|Mitarashi|ja|御手洗場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitarashi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic">Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.112</ref>.
Despite being a shrine without a {{ill|shaden (shrine building)|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shaden (shrine building)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, it receives rituals equivalent to those of a {{ill|Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja|別宮|lt=Betsugū|1=Setsumatsusha|2=ja|3=摂末社|4=zh|5=攝末社|6=es|7=Setsumatsusha|8=fr|9=Setsumatsusha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Betsugū (auxiliary shrine)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making it a special shrine within the Naikū precincts<ref name="ic"/>.
== Overview ==
Situated in {{ill|Ujikanda-chō|ja|宇治館町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujikanda-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 宇治 (伊勢市)|1=Uji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11449448}}, [[:en:Ise, Mie|Ise City]], Mie Prefecture, within the grounds of Naikū, Takimatsuri-no-Kami is Ranked first among the 30 shrines under Naikū's jurisdiction. As the enshrined deity is the [[:en:Suijin|water god]] of the [[:en:Isuzu River|Isuzu River (Mie)]], which symbolizes the location of Naikū, rituals similar to those of auxiliary shrines are conducted<ref name="ic"/>. The {{ill|Ujiyamada City History|ja|宇治山田市史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada City History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes:
{{Cquote|Since ancient times, Takimatsuri-no-Kami has been honored with offerings akin to those presented at auxiliary shrines, thus Ranked particularly between the auxiliary shrines and the associate shrines.}}
Locals affectionately refer to it as "Otoritsugi-san" (おとりつぎさん)<ref name="ic"/>. It is believed that praying to Takimatsuri-no-Kami before visiting the main shrine will help convey one’s wishes to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk">Gakken Publishing (2013): p.55</ref>.
There is no shrine building; instead, a sacred stone is enshrined within the [[:en:Itagaki|Itagaki (sacred fence)]]<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>. The reason for the absence of a shrine building remains unknown even to those involved with Ise Jingu. The mystery deepens with the offering of purification rituals, and even the "Daijingū Gishiki-kai" (1775) only states, "It has profound significance"<ref>Hozumi (2013): pp.160–161</ref>. While most shrines under Ise Jingu do not have offering boxes<ref>Ise Bunka-sha (2008): p.26</ref>, Takimatsuri-no-Kami does have one.
== Enshrined Deity ==
The enshrined deity is '''Takimatsuri-no-Ōkami''' (瀧祭大神)<ref name="ic"/>. This deity represents the {{ill|source of the Isuzu River|ja|五十鈴川の水源|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/source of the Isuzu River|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}<ref name="ic"/> and is worshipped for the purpose of {{ill|flood control|ja|治水|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood control|qq=}}, as it sits at the confluence of the Isuzu and Shimaji Rivers<ref name="ic"/><ref name="gk"/>.
== History ==
The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Enryaku Gishikichō|ja|延暦儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enryaku Gishikichō|qq=}} as "Takimatsuri Shrine" and "Takimatsuri no Chi" (滝祭の地)<ref name="gs">Sakurai (1970): p.165</ref>. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} or the [[:en:Rikkokushi|Rikkokushi]], making it an {{ill|shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|ja|式外社|1=Shikigeisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shikigai-sha (non-listed shrine)|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikigeisha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}<ref name="gs"/>.
According to the {{ill|Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja|建久年中行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkyū Annual Rituals|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, water from the Isuzu River was drawn at "Takimatsuri no Se" and delivered to the [[:en:Saikū|Saikū]] in bamboo tubes during special festivals<ref name="gs3">Sakurai (1970): p.120</ref>. While the exact location of "Takimatsuri no Se" is unknown, it is estimated to be somewhere between the first [[:en:Torii|torii]] and Takimatsuri-no-Kami<ref name="gs3"/>.
The practice of {{ill|offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|ja|奉幣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/offering sacred cloth (hōbei)|1=Hōhei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11445508}} began during the Meiji period<ref>Sakurai (1970): pp.165–166</ref>.
== Rituals ==
The '''Yuki-no-Ōmike''' (由貴大御饌) ceremony is performed by opening the sacred gate within the Itagaki fence<ref name="gs"/>. The sacred rice offerings are cultivated in the sacred rice fields of Ise Jingu and stored in the {{ill|Mishine-no-Mikura|ja|御稲御倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mishine-no-Mikura|qq=}}, the same as those dedicated to Naikū and {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}}<ref>Sakurai (1970): p.156</ref>.
During the [[:en:Kannamesai Festival|Kannamesai]], a great banquet ({{ill|Naorai|ja|直会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naorai|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) is held following the rituals, followed by Takimatsuri ceremonies<ref name="gs2">Sakurai (1970): p.166</ref>. After the Yuki-no-Ōmike and Takimatsuri ceremonies, another Naorai is conducted<ref name="gs2"/>. The post-Takimatsuri Naorai was an important ritual, as only two individuals, the {{ill|negi (Shinto priest)|ja|禰宜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/negi (Shinto priest)|1=Negi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11594490}} and Uji Ōuchi-no-hito, were permitted to partake in newly harvested rice and sake<ref name="gs2"/>.
== Access ==
; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujibashi Bridge|1=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997081}} * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit (JR side) of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|JR Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>. Also accessible via bus from [[:en:Isuzugawa Station|Isuzugawa Station]] on the Kintetsu Toba Line. * About 5 minutes south via {{ill|Mie Prefectural Route 32|ja|三重県道32号伊勢磯部線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mie Prefectural Route 32|1=Mie Prefectural Road Route 32|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11357920}} (Mikimoto Road) from {{ill|Ise-nishi Interchange|ja|伊勢西インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise-nishi Interchange|1=Ise-nishi itnerchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884865}} on the [[:en:Ise Expressway|Ise Expressway]], or from {{ill|Ise Interchange|ja|伊勢インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Interchange|1=Ise interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10884857}} via [[:en:Japan National Route 23|National Route 23 (Japan)]]. Parking available nearby<ref name="mkr"/>.
; From Ujibashi Bridge * Cross Ujibashi, proceed through the sacred grounds (Shinen), pass over the {{ill|Hiyoke Bridge|ja|火除橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiyoke Bridge|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and through the first torii gate of Naikū to reach the Mitarashi area<ref name="ic"/>. Takimatsuri-no-Kami is located just south of the Mitarashi, nestled within a grove<ref name="gs"/>.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|List of 125 Shrines of Ise|ja|神宮125社の一覧|lt=List of 125 Shrines of Ise|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|3=伊勢国の式内社一覧|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of 125 Shrines of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Ise Province|qq=}}

; To {{ill|Ujibashi Bridge|ja|宇治橋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujibashi Bridge|1=Uji Bridge (Ise City)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q23997081}} * About 15 minutes by [[:en:Mie Kotsu|Mie Kotsu]] bus from the south exit (JR side) of [[:en:Iseshi Station|Ise Station]] on the [[:en:Sangū Line|JR Sangū Line]] or from {{ill|Ujiyamada Station|ja|Ujiyamada Station|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiyamada Station|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on the Kintetsu Yamada and Toba Lines, alighting at the final stop "Naikū-mae"<ref name="mkr">Mie Prefecture Tourism Federation, "[http://www.kankomie.or.jp/spot/detail_2937.html Ise Jingu (Naikū) Tourist Info - Visit Mie]" (Accessed October 12, 2013)</ref>.
